title: A De Novo Variant as a Candidate Driver in a Neurodevelopmental Disorder: Insights from Trio-Based Whole-Exome Sequencing.
abstract: Trio WES case report of a female with ASD level 1, borderline intellectual functioning, coordination disorder, and epilepsy. Identifies a de novo USP24 variant (c.3155G>T; p.Ser1052Ile) classified as likely pathogenic by ACMG. Two paternally inherited VUS in additional NDD-implicated genes. Authors support a cumulative variant model rather than strict monogenic causation.
methods: Trio whole-exome sequencing, ACMG classification, array-CGH
summary: Trio WES case report of a female with ASD level 1, borderline intellectual functioning, coordination disorder, and epilepsy. Identifies a de novo USP24 variant (c.3155G>T; p.Ser1052Ile) classified as likely pathogenic by ACMG. Two paternally inherited VUS in additional NDD-implicated genes. Authors
